# ADR-005 Compliance — Application Architecture

**Governance phase:** 3.5 · **Authority document:** `docs/ADR-005-*.md` · **Date:** 2026-07-19
**Verifier:** `architecture/compliance/verify-architecture.mjs` · **Do not edit the ADR itself.**

## Purpose
Define the acting layer: five one-way layers; one use case per capability; command=one transaction boundary; gates before acts; events as outcomes.

## Scope
Business application architecture — layers, use cases, workflow/compensation, events. No implementation prescription.

## Current implementation status
**Proposed; Realized for migrated contexts — 45% compliant.**

## Implemented components
- Five layers with one-way deps (Presentation→Application→Domain; Infra implements ports)
- Ports defined by Application, asserted at composition (fail-fast)
- Typed results; queries never mutate
- Gates (authorization/validation) before domain logic
- Command serialization via dbTransaction (C-1 collision fixed)

## Remaining work
- Cross-context processes with events + compensation
- Full C-1 isolation (connection-per-tx / Postgres)
- Migrate remaining contexts to this shape

## Evidence
- verifier R1–R7 PASS (26 files)
- Identity 35/35 + Users 17/17 A/B
- C-1 concurrent-completion live test (both 200)

## Related source code
- `src/domain/**`
- `src/application/**`
- `src/infrastructure/**`
- `src/presentation/**`
- `src/config/database.js`

## Related tests
- `tests/unit/*.test.js`
- `tests/integration/*-ab.mjs`

## Compliance percentage
**45%** — honest estimate: weight of ADR requirements realized under the ADR-005
layered architecture with evidence, vs. total requirements named in the ADR.

## Risks
- C-1 fully isolated only with connection-per-tx/Postgres (ADR-001)
- 6 of 11 contexts still legacy shape

## Required actions
- Continue strangler migration under A/B + verifier gate
- Land ADR-001 isolation
